description_long: This factory building complex consists of several interconnected and separate structures, including a long, curved main building with repetitive window patterns and a central entrance, as well as three auxiliary rectangular buildings. The main building features a flat roof and a utilitarian facade, with large industrial windows running along its length. One of the auxiliary buildings stands out with a blue-tinted glass exterior, suggesting an office or administrative function. The overall construction is predominantly concrete and glass, with visible rooftop installations such as vents and possible HVAC units. The site layout is open, with clear separations between the buildings, typical of industrial campuses designed for vehicle and freight access. The architectural style is functional and modern industrial, prioritizing practicality and efficiency.
description: A multi-building factory complex with a long, curved main structure featuring large industrial windows and several auxiliary buildings, including a blue-glass office block. The design is utilitarian, with flat roofs and visible rooftop machinery.
description_view_0: The main curved factory building is prominent, with the blue-glass office building and two smaller auxiliary structures spaced apart.
description_view_1: The long side of the main building is visible, showing the repetitive window pattern, with the blue-glass building in the background.
description_view_2: The rear of the main building is seen, with the auxiliary buildings more separated and the blue-glass office block to the right.
description_view_3: A frontal perspective of the complex, highlighting the main building's entrance and the blue-glass office building in the foreground.
